title: `quiver:cost` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver cost` that estimates the token footprint of each slice's context bundle (AGENTS.md + QUICK.md + PROJECT_MAP.md + declared files) as a range, with an optional tokenizer-backed path when `tiktoken` or `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer` is installed.
description: v14 introduced the tiered context pack and token budgets in AGENTS.md, but there is no tool to audit whether a slice respects the budget. This command scans each pending slice, sums the characters of its context bundle, and prints both a heuristic range (using `chars/4`) and, when available, a precise count from an installed tokenizer. Over-budget warnings fire against the numeric budget declared in AGENTS.md.
must: Implement heuristic counter `chars/4` with an explicit disclaimer in output
Detect optional tokenizers at runtime: try `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer`, then `tiktoken`, fallback to heuristic
Parse AGENTS.md for declared per-mode token budgets; warn when a slice exceeds them
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Automatically rewriting or shrinking context files
Per-model token counting for non-Anthropic models beyond the optional tokenizers
Network calls to a tokenizer API
acceptance: `npx create-quiver cost` prints per-slice ranges and a disclaimer that the heuristic is `chars/4`
With `@anthropic-ai/tokenizer` installed, output uses the tokenizer and notes the source
A slice whose bundle exceeds the budget in AGENTS.md triggers a `WARN: over-budget` line
`--json` output is valid JSON including `tokens_low`, `tokens_high`, and `over_budget: boolean`
tests: node bin/create-quiver.js cost --json | node -e "const c=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync(0)); if(!Array.isArray(c.slices)) process.exit(1)"
assumptions: AGENTS.md "Reading Budget" section is parseable enough to extract per-mode numeric budgets
`chars/4` is within ±40% accurate, enough for over-budget warnings
Optional tokenizers do not need to match every model exactly; they raise confidence
